distractorExplanations.d: Escrow instructions replace need for agreement.

options.text: Contract setting terms for transfer of property from seller to buyer.
A bilateral contract contains mutual promises by both parties, such as a buyer promising to buy and a seller promising to sell under agreed terms.
An executory contract is still awaiting performance by one or more parties, such as a signed purchase agreement before closing and deed delivery.
Valid until party with right to rescind avoids it.

explanation: Contract setting terms for transfer of property from seller to buyer. Example: Residential purchase agreement includes price, contingencies, closing terms.

distractorExplanations.b: Only one party has obligation.
distractorExplanations.c: Executory means invalid.
distractorExplanations.d: Voidable and void are the same.

options.text: Unqualified agreement to terms of offer communicated as required.
Proposal to enter contract on definite terms communicated to offeree.
Contract setting terms for transfer of property from seller to buyer.
Canceling contract and restoring parties to pre-contract positions.

explanation: Canceling contract and restoring parties to pre-contract positions. Example: Buyer rescinds due to misrepresentation.

distractorExplanations.a: Counteroffer is acceptance.
distractorExplanations.b: Invitation to negotiate is always offer.
distractorExplanations.c: Escrow instructions replace need for agreement.

prompt: Who is beneficiary in deed of trust?

options.text: Lender who benefits from deed of trust security.
Process of taking, processing, underwriting, and funding a loan.
Loan balance increases because payments are less than interest due.
Loan with interest rate unchanged for loan term.

explanation: Lender who benefits from deed of trust security. Example: Bank lender is beneficiary.

distractorExplanations.b: Origination is same as servicing.
distractorExplanations.c: Thinking every adjustable loan negatively amortizes.
distractorExplanations.d: Payment never changes for taxes/insurance escrow.

prompt: What is a deed of trust?

options.text: Trustee releases deed of trust lien after debt is paid.
Process of taking, processing, underwriting, and funding a loan.
Security instrument involving borrower/trustor, lender/beneficiary, and trustee holding power of sale.
Settlement procedures, disclosures, and kickbacks/referral fees for many residential mortgage transactions.

explanation: Security instrument involving borrower/trustor, lender/beneficiary, and trustee holding power of sale. Example: California commonly uses deed of trust.

distractorExplanations.a: Confusing reconveyance with grant deed sale.
distractorExplanations.b: Origination is same as servicing.
distractorExplanations.d: RESPA is primarily fair lending law.

options.text: Foreclosure under power of sale without court action, common with deeds of trust.
Loan amount divided by property value or price.
Borrower who gives security interest in property.
Loan meeting Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac standards including loan limits.

explanation: Loan amount divided by property value or price. Formula: $$LTV=\frac{Loan\ Amount}{Value}$$ Example: Loan 400k on 500k home is 80% LTV.

distractorExplanations.a: No notice required because no court.
distractorExplanations.c: Confusing trustor with trustee.
distractorExplanations.d: Conforming means FHA insured.

prompt: What is an ARM rate cap?

options.text: Limit on how much interest rate can change per adjustment or over life of loan.
Market where existing loans are sold to investors or agencies, adding liquidity to lenders.
Loan with rate adjusting based on index, margin, and adjustment rules.
Loan meeting Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac standards including loan limits.

explanation: Limit on how much interest rate can change per adjustment or over life of loan. Example: 2/6 cap limits periodic/lifetime increase.

distractorExplanations.b: Secondary market makes loans to borrowers directly.
distractorExplanations.c: Ignoring caps.
distractorExplanations.d: Conforming means FHA insured.

prompt: How does mortgage differ from deed of trust (Drill 2)?

options.text: Loan not insured or guaranteed by government.
Mortgage involves borrower and lender and often judicial foreclosure; deed of trust includes trustee and power of sale.
Foreclosure under power of sale without court action, common with deeds of trust.
Loan guaranteed by Department of Veterans Affairs for eligible veterans/service members.

explanation: Mortgage involves borrower and lender and often judicial foreclosure; deed of trust includes trustee and power of sale. Example: Some states use mortgages more commonly than deeds of trust.

distractorExplanations.a: Conventional means unregulated.
distractorExplanations.c: No notice required because no court.
distractorExplanations.d: VA always requires down payment.

options.text: Deed with covenants/warranties of title, more common in some states than California practice.
Neutral third party holds documents/funds and follows written instructions to close transaction.
Written instrument used to transfer title to real property.
Actual notice is direct knowledge of a property fact, such as personally knowing about an easement, tenant right, lien, or defect even if it is not discovered through recording.

explanation: Neutral third party holds documents/funds and follows written instructions to close transaction. Example: Escrow holder delivers deed when conditions met.

distractorExplanations.a: Assuming California commonly uses warranty deed in standard sale.
distractorExplanations.c: A deed and title are the same thing.
distractorExplanations.d: Ignoring known facts because not recorded.

options.text: Neutral third party holds documents/funds and follows written instructions to close transaction.
Actual notice is direct knowledge of a property fact, such as personally knowing about an easement, tenant right, lien, or defect even if it is not discovered through recording.
Deed must be delivered by grantor and accepted by grantee to transfer title.
Dividing expenses/income between buyer and seller as of closing date.

explanation: Dividing expenses/income between buyer and seller as of closing date. Example: Property taxes prorated at closing.

distractorExplanations.a: Escrow represents buyer or seller as advocate.
distractorExplanations.b: Ignoring known facts because not recorded.
distractorExplanations.c: Signing alone always transfers title.

prompt: What is a California grant deed?

options.text: Acquiring title by open, notorious, hostile, continuous, exclusive possession for statutory period plus required conditions such as taxes in California.
Competent grantor, identifiable grantee, granting clause, legal description, execution, and delivery/acceptance.
Deed implying grantor has not previously conveyed title and has not encumbered property except disclosed.
Actual notice is direct knowledge of a property fact, such as personally knowing about an easement, tenant right, lien, or defect even if it is not discovered through recording.

explanation: Deed implying grantor has not previously conveyed title and has not encumbered property except disclosed. Example: Seller conveys property by grant deed.

distractorExplanations.a: Simply using land occasionally creates title.
distractorExplanations.b: Recording is required for validity between parties.
distractorExplanations.d: Ignoring known facts because not recorded.

prompt: Why are delivery and acceptance important?

options.text: Court-supervised process of administering estate and transferring property.
Deed must be delivered by grantor and accepted by grantee to transfer title.
Transfer by valid will after death through probate process.
Actual notice is direct knowledge of a property fact, such as personally knowing about an easement, tenant right, lien, or defect even if it is not discovered through recording.

explanation: Deed must be delivered by grantor and accepted by grantee to transfer title. Example: Escrow delivers deed at closing.

distractorExplanations.a: Probate is required for every property transfer.
distractorExplanations.c: Will avoids every probate issue automatically.
distractorExplanations.d: Ignoring known facts because not recorded.

options.text: Placing document in public records to give constructive notice and establish priority.
Transfer by valid will after death through probate process.
Dividing expenses/income between buyer and seller as of closing date.
Court-supervised process of administering estate and transferring property.

explanation: Court-supervised process of administering estate and transferring property. Example: Executor sells estate property with authority.

distractorExplanations.a: Unrecorded deed is always invalid.
distractorExplanations.b: Will avoids every probate issue automatically.
distractorExplanations.c: Charging entire annual tax to one side without period allocation.
